title: agoseris glauca var. parviflora
intro: Agoseris glauca var. parviflora, also known as smallflower tickseed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the western regions of North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from respiratory symptoms, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Agoseris glauca var. parviflora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
